reasoning: To be the #1 most streamed Spotify artist for the entire year of 2025, an artist would need to accumulate massive, sustained daily streams, typically well over 50-60 million streams daily on average. Taylor Swift, Bad Bunny, and The Weeknd consistently dominate this metric, taking the top spots year over year. While Eminem is a legendary artist and consistently ranks high (often top 15 in monthly listeners), he does not generate the sheer volume of global, daily streams required to beat out the current pop and Latin music titans for an entire calendar year. Tracking data from early 2025 (e.g. late January global top 10) lists artists like Bad Bunny, Taylor Swift, The Weeknd, Bruno Mars, Drake, Billie Eilish, SZA, Kendrick Lamar, Ariana Grande, and Peso Pluma, without Eminem breaking into those top ranks. Therefore, the probability of him being the #1 most streamed artist of 2025 is exceedingly low. I assign a 2% chance in the event of an unprecedented shock to his catalog's stream volume or catastrophic drops for the top 10, neither of which is likely.
